# slidekit — Full Documentation for LLMs

This single file contains the complete slidekit documentation, inlined for LLM
consumption. A concise index is in `docs/llms.txt`.

slidekit is an HTTP service (and single self-contained binary) that turns Markdown
into **one fully self-contained Slidev HTML deck**: all CSS, JavaScript and fonts
are inlined, there are zero external requests, it works offline from `file://`, and
it includes Slidev presenter mode. It renders *exactly* like Slidev because it runs
Slidev's real build.

- Source: https://github.com/mikebild/slidekit
- License: MIT

---

## 1. Core concept

You POST Markdown to `/render`; slidekit runs a real `slidev build`, inlines
everything into a single `index.html`, strips any external references, injects
metadata, and returns that one file. The output is ~2–6 MB and needs no server to
view — open it in a browser, present with `…#/presenter/1`.

```
Markdown ──POST /render──▶ slidev build ──▶ inline CSS/JS/fonts ──▶ one .html
```

What it is NOT: it is not a hosting platform and it does not store decks. It
transforms input to output. Themes, fonts and config are bundled; your Markdown is
the only input.

---

## 2. HTTP API

Base URL (self-host): `http://localhost:4030`.

### Endpoints

| Method | Path | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| GET  | `/`               | JSON service descriptor (links to render, openapi, llms) |
| POST | `/render`         | Markdown → one self-contained HTML deck |
| GET  | `/themes`         | JSON array of available CSS-overlay theme names |
| GET  | `/openapi.json`   | Generated OpenAPI 3.1 spec |
| GET  | `/health`         | Liveness — `200 "ok"` while the process is up |
| GET  | `/ready`          | Readiness — `200 {"status":"ready"}`; `503 {"status":"draining"}` during shutdown |
| GET  | `/metrics`        | Prometheus metrics (requests, builds, durations, in-flight, cache) |
| GET  | `/jobs/{id}`      | Async job status (queued/running/done/error) |
| GET  | `/jobs/{id}/result` | Async job result — the self-contained HTML deck once done |
| GET  | `/llms.txt`       | This documentation index (llmstxt.org format) |
| GET  | `/llms-full.txt`  | The full documentation inlined in one file |

### POST /render

- **Body**: the raw Markdown with `Content-Type: text/markdown`, OR
  `multipart/form-data` with a file field named `file`, `markdown`, or `md`.
- **Auth**: when the server has `SLIDEKIT_API_KEYS` configured, send the key as
  `Authorization: Bearer <key>` or `X-API-Key: <key>`. If no keys are configured,
  the endpoint is open (not recommended in production — see Security).
- **Query parameters** (all optional):

| Param | Effect |
|---|---|
| `theme` | CSS overlay name (see `/themes`); unknown → server default (`neutral`) |
| `title` | sets `<title>` + `og:title`/`twitter:title` + the download filename |
| `author` | `meta[name=author]` |
| `tags` | comma-separated → `meta[name=keywords]` |
| `description` | meta description + OpenGraph/Twitter description |
| `image` | social image URL → `og:image`/`twitter:image` |
| `imageAlt` | alt text → `og:image:alt`/`twitter:image:alt` (only with `image`) |
| `url` | canonical URL → `og:url` + `<link rel=canonical>` |
| `siteName` | `og:site_name` |
| `locale` | `og:locale` (e.g. `en_US`) |
| `lang` | sets `<html lang>` (a11y/SEO) |
| `robots` | `meta[name=robots]` (e.g. `noindex`) |
| `themeColor` | `meta[name=theme-color]` (browser UI tint) |
| `twitterSite` / `twitterCreator` | `twitter:site` / `twitter:creator` (@handles) |
| `favicon` | inline favicon: an emoji/short text (rendered to SVG) or a `data:` URI — stays offline |
| `download=1` | respond with `Content-Disposition: attachment` |
| `async=1` | enqueue the build; respond `202` + a job to poll at `/jobs/{id}` |
| `callback` | async only: `POST {id,status}` to this URL on completion (host must be allowlisted via `SLIDEKIT_WEBHOOK_ALLOW`) |

Successful `200` responses carry a strong `ETag`; a matching `If-None-Match`
request gets `304 Not Modified`. Identical renders (same Markdown + theme) are
served from an in-memory cache without re-building. Large text/JSON responses
are gzip/deflate-compressed when the client sends `Accept-Encoding`.

- **Responses**:

| Status | Meaning |
|---|---|
| 200 | `text/html` — the self-contained deck (attachment when `download=1`); `ETag` set |
| 202 | async accepted — `{id,status}` JSON + `Location: /jobs/{id}` (with `async=1`) |
| 304 | `If-None-Match` matched the current `ETag` |
| 400 | empty Markdown, or `callback` host not allowlisted |
| 401 | missing/invalid API key (when auth is enabled) |
| 413 | body exceeds `SLIDEKIT_MAX_BODY_BYTES` |
| 429 | rate limit exceeded (headers `RateLimit-*`, `Retry-After`) |
| 500 | build failed (server logs the real reason) |
| 503 | build queue full or queue wait timed out |
| 504 | build timed out |

### Async jobs — GET /jobs/{id} and /jobs/{id}/result

With `POST /render?async=1` the build is enqueued and a job is returned
immediately (`202`). Poll `GET /jobs/{id}` for `{ "id", "status" }` where status
is `queued` / `running` / `done` / `error`; when `done` it includes
`"result": "/jobs/{id}/result"`. `GET /jobs/{id}/result` returns the deck HTML
(`200`, with `ETag`), `409` while the job is not finished, or `404` if the job
is unknown/expired/failed. Both job routes require the API key when auth is
enabled. Optionally pass `callback=<url>` to receive a completion webhook; the
callback host must be listed in `SLIDEKIT_WEBHOOK_ALLOW` (empty ⇒ webhooks off).

### Examples

Render and download (with a theme + metadata):

```bash
curl --data-binary @deck.md -H 'content-type: text/markdown' \
  -H 'authorization: Bearer sk_…' \
  'http://localhost:4030/render?theme=editorial&title=My%20Deck&author=Me&download=1' \
  -o deck.html
```

Multipart upload:

```bash
curl -F file=@deck.md -H 'authorization: Bearer sk_…' \
  'http://localhost:4030/render?theme=neutral' -o deck.html
```

List themes / fetch spec:

```bash
curl http://localhost:4030/themes        # ["editorial","neutral"]
curl http://localhost:4030/openapi.json
```

The returned HTML: open it directly (offline). Presenter mode with notes + next
slide is at `deck.html#/presenter/1` (hash routing is injected automatically).

---

## 3. The two-layer theming model

slidekit has TWO unrelated "theme" concepts. Confusing them is the most common
mistake:

- **`?theme=` (query param / upload field)** = a slidekit **CSS overlay**
  (`neutral`, `editorial`, or any file in the themes directory). This controls the
  look (palette, typography, accent).
- **`theme:` (Markdown frontmatter)** = a **Slidev npm theme package**
  (e.g. `seriph`, `default`). slidekit pins this and **normalizes whatever your
  deck specifies** to `SLIDEKIT_BASE_THEME` (default `seriph`).

> Important: putting `theme: neutral` (an overlay name) — or any uninstalled
> theme — in your frontmatter does NOT break the build. slidekit rewrites the
> top-level `theme:` to the base theme before building. Pick the visual look with
> `?theme=`, not with frontmatter.

```
your Markdown
  └─ Slidev base theme   (fixed: SLIDEKIT_BASE_THEME = seriph)
       └─ CSS overlay     (per request: ?theme=neutral | editorial | …)
            └─ one self-contained HTML
```

### Built-in overlays

| Overlay | Look | Accent (light / dark) |
|---|---|---|
| `neutral` (default) | shadcn-style neutral; white / near-black | teal `#14b8a6` / `#2dd4bf` |
| `editorial` | warm paper / espresso; bolder headings | indigo `#4f46e5` / `#818cf8` |

`GET /themes` lists what's available; the OpenAPI `theme` enum is populated
from it.

### Design tokens

Each overlay defines CSS variables on `:root` (light) and `html.dark` (dark):

| Token | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `--c-bg` | slide background |
| `--c-fg` | foreground / text |
| `--c-muted` | muted/secondary text |
| `--c-border` | card & divider borders (class `.border-token`) |
| `--c-accent` | accent (links, icons, highlights) |
| `--slidev-theme-primary` | Slidev primary var, set to `--c-accent` |

Overlays also remap authored accent utilities onto `--c-accent`:
`text-teal-400/500/600`, `border-teal-500/600` and `bg-teal-9xx`. So a deck
written with `text-teal-500` automatically follows whichever overlay is active.

### Light & dark

Add `colorSchema: all` to the deck frontmatter to ship both palettes and the
in-deck light/dark toggle (Slidev toolbar). The output is self-contained, so the
toggle works offline.

### Fonts (offline)

Fonts are base64-inlined (`fonts.css`: Inter + JetBrains Mono) → zero external
font requests. Set `fonts: { provider: none }` in your deck so Slidev doesn't add
a Google Fonts `<link>` (the server also strips external `<link>`s as a safety
net). Regenerate `fonts.css` with `node gen-fonts.mjs`.

### Writing a custom overlay theme

Create `themes/<name>.css`; it appears automatically in `/themes` and the
OpenAPI enum. For the binary, rebuild (`npm run build:binary`) or set
`SLIDEKIT_THEMES_DIR` to an external folder. Template:

```css
/* Theme: mybrand */
@import '../fonts.css';
:root {
  --c-bg: #ffffff; --c-fg: #0b1020; --c-muted: #5b6472; --c-border: #e6e8ee;
  --c-accent: #2563eb; --slidev-theme-primary: var(--c-accent);
}
html.dark {
  --c-bg: #0b1020; --c-fg: #e8ebf2; --c-muted: #9aa3b2; --c-border: #222a3a;
  --c-accent: #60a5fa;
}
.slidev-layout { background-color: var(--c-bg); color: var(--c-fg); font-family: 'Inter', ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif; }
.slidev-layout.cover, .slidev-layout.intro { background-image: none !important; background-color: var(--c-bg) !important; color: var(--c-fg) !important; }
.slidev-layout :is(h1,h2,h3,h4) { color: var(--c-fg); font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -0.02em; }
.border-token { border-color: var(--c-border) !important; }
.slidev-layout a { color: var(--c-accent); }
.slidev-layout :is(.text-teal-400,.text-teal-500,.text-teal-600) { color: var(--c-accent) !important; }
.slidev-layout :is(.border-teal-500,.border-teal-600) { border-color: var(--c-accent) !important; }
.slidev-layout :is([class*="bg-teal-9"]) { background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--c-accent) 10%, transparent) !important; }
.slidev-layout svg { vertical-align: -0.18em; }
```

---

## 4. Icons

Icons render via Iconify (the SVG is inlined; no network at view time). Write them
as components: `<{prefix}-{icon-name} />`.

### Bundled collections

| Collection | Prefix | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Material Symbols | `material-symbols` | primary set (16k+), explicit dependency — guaranteed |
| Carbon | `carbon` | bundled via Slidev's default theme |
| Phosphor | `ph` | bundled via Slidev |
| Spinners | `svg-spinners` | animated loaders, bundled via Slidev |

Only `material-symbols` is a direct dependency; the rest arrive transitively with
Slidev. Use an installed collection — an uninstalled one fails the build (same
failure mode as an unknown theme).

### Usage & styling

```md
<material-symbols-key-outline />
<material-symbols-link class="text-teal-500 text-2xl" />
<carbon-rocket />
```

- Material Symbols variants: append `-outline`, `-rounded`, `-sharp` (bare = filled).
- Color via `text-*` (icons use `currentColor`); size via `text-*`. Coloring an
  icon `text-teal-500` makes it follow the active theme accent.
- Find names: https://icones.js.org or https://icon-sets.iconify.design.
- MDC inline form (decks with `mdc: true`):
  `… :material-symbols-lock-outline{.text-teal-500} …` in prose. Inside a raw
  `<div>`, use the component form, not `:icon{}`.
- Add another collection: `npm i @iconify-json/<name>`, rebuild the binary, then
  `<lucide-rocket />`.

---

## 5. Authoring decks — checklist

- `colorSchema: all` for the light/dark toggle.
- `fonts: { provider: none }` to stay fully offline.
- Don't rely on `theme:` — it's normalized; choose the look via `?theme=`.
- Use `text-teal-*` for accents so they follow the chosen overlay.
- Use bundled icons (`material-symbols`, `carbon`, `ph`, `svg-spinners`).
- All standard Slidev features work: layouts (`two-cols`, `::right::`),
  `v-clicks`, `mermaid`, KaTeX, UnoCSS/Tailwind utility classes, transitions.

Example:

```md
---
layout: two-cols
colorSchema: all
fonts: { provider: none }
---

# Before
<v-clicks>

- <material-symbols-link class="text-teal-500" /> APIs
- <material-symbols-lock-outline class="text-teal-500" /> Secrets

</v-clicks>

::right::

# After
\`\`\`mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[Markdown] --> B[slidekit] --> C[one HTML]
\`\`\`
```

---

## 6. Configuration (environment variables)

All config is via env vars (optionally a `.env` file; loaded by Node's built-in
loader). Invalid values fail fast at startup.

| Variable | Purpose | Default |
|---|---|---|
| `PORT` | HTTP listen port | `4030` |
| `HOST` | bind address (`127.0.0.1` behind a proxy) | `0.0.0.0` |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | `debug`/`info`/`warn`/`error` | `info` |
| `LOG_FORMAT` | `json`/`pretty` | `json` |
| `SLIDEKIT_API_KEYS` | comma-separated; non-empty ⇒ `/render` requires a key | (empty) |
| `SLIDEKIT_REQUIRE_AUTH_ALL` | `1` ⇒ require a key on all routes except `/health`,`/ready` | `0` |
| `SLIDEKIT_TRUST_PROXY` | `1` ⇒ client IP from `X-Forwarded-For` | `0` |
| `SLIDEKIT_RATE_LIMIT_MAX` | max `/render` req per window per identity; `0` disables | `30` |
| `SLIDEKIT_RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW_MS` | rate-limit window (ms) | `60000` |
| `SLIDEKIT_MAX_BODY_BYTES` | max upload size → 413 | `1048576` |
| `SLIDEKIT_BUILD_TIMEOUT_MS` | kill a build (whole process group) → 504 | `120000` |
| `SLIDEKIT_BUILD_CONCURRENCY` | concurrent builds (kept at 1) | `1` |
| `SLIDEKIT_BUILD_QUEUE_MAX` | queued builds before 503 | `20` |
| `SLIDEKIT_QUEUE_TIMEOUT_MS` | max time a request waits in the build queue → 503 | `30000` |
| `SLIDEKIT_JANITOR_INTERVAL_MS` | periodic sweep of stale temp files; `0` disables | `0` |
| `SLIDEKIT_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS` | `http.Server.requestTimeout` (slow-loris guard); `0` off | `130000` |
| `SLIDEKIT_HEADERS_TIMEOUT_MS` | `http.Server.headersTimeout`; `0` off | `15000` |
| `SLIDEKIT_CACHE_MAX` | render-cache entries (same Markdown+theme); `0` disables | `50` |
| `SLIDEKIT_CACHE_TTL_MS` | render-cache entry TTL; `0` = no expiry | `0` |
| `SLIDEKIT_JOBS_MAX` | max retained async jobs/results | `100` |
| `SLIDEKIT_JOB_TTL_MS` | async job/result lifetime | `600000` |
| `SLIDEKIT_WEBHOOK_ALLOW` | comma-separated host allowlist for `callback`; empty ⇒ webhooks off | (empty) |
| `SLIDEKIT_COMPRESSION` | gzip/deflate/br compress responses; `0` disables | `1` |
| `SLIDEKIT_CORS_ORIGIN` | value for `Access-Control-Allow-Origin`; empty ⇒ no CORS | (empty) |
| `SLIDEKIT_DEFAULT_THEME` | overlay when `?theme=` is absent/unknown | `neutral` |
| `SLIDEKIT_BASE_THEME` | Slidev base theme; deck `theme:` is normalized to this | `seriph` |
| `SLIDEKIT_THEMES_DIR` | directory of `*.css` overlays | `<root>/themes` |
| `SLIDEKIT_WORK_DIR` | scratch dir for build outputs | `<tmpdir>/slidekit` |
| `SLIDEKIT_SHUTDOWN_GRACE_MS` | drain time for the in-flight build on SIGTERM | `10000` |

---

## 7. Running & deploying

- **Node service**: `npm ci && node server.mjs` (or `npm run serve`).
- **Single binary**: `npm run build:binary` → `dist/slidekit-deck` (embeds the
  whole runtime; extracts to `~/.cache/slidekit-deck/<hash>` on first run; no
  Docker, no external `node_modules`). Platform-specific — build per OS/arch.
  Prebuilt macOS-arm64 / Linux-x64/arm64 binaries are attached to GitHub Releases.
- **Docker**: `docker build -t slidekit . && docker run -p 4030:4030 -e SLIDEKIT_API_KEYS=$KEYS slidekit` (non-root, healthchecked).

Lifecycle: `/health` (liveness), `/ready` (readiness, 503 while draining). On
SIGTERM the service fails readiness, drains the in-flight build, then exits 0.
Logs are structured JSON to stdout (one line per request, with a request id).

---

## 8. Security / trust model (read before exposing)

`/render` runs a real Slidev/Vite build on the uploaded Markdown. A Slidev deck
can contain `<script setup>`, Vue components and Vite config that **execute during
the build**. Therefore:

> Uploading Markdown is equivalent to running arbitrary code as the server's OS
> user. This is inherent to "render exactly like Slidev" and cannot be sandboxed
> in-process.

Built-in controls (API keys, rate limit, body-size cap, build timeout, concurrency
+ queue caps) are defense-in-depth, NOT a sandbox. Deploy safely: require
`SLIDEKIT_API_KEYS`, put it behind a reverse proxy with auth, bind `HOST=127.0.0.1`,
and run non-root in an ephemeral, egress-restricted container/VM. Report
vulnerabilities privately to mike@mikebild.com.

---

## 9. Architecture (for contributors)

Zero runtime dependencies in the server code (Node built-ins only); rendering is
delegated to the bundled Slidev toolchain. Request lifecycle for `POST /render`:

```
security headers + (optional) API-key auth + rate limit + body-size cap
  → resolve theme + parse metadata from query
  → forceBaseTheme(md)  (normalize deck theme: to the base theme)
  → ensureHashRouting(md)  (offline + presenter without a server)
  → build.run(): queue → swap style.css to the overlay → real `slidev build`
  → read the single inlined index.html (vite-plugin-singlefile)
  → stripExternals(html)  (remove favicon + Google Fonts links)
  → injectMeta(html, meta)  (title + OpenGraph/Twitter, deduped)
  → one self-contained HTML
```

Modules under `src/`: `config`, `logger`, `server` (composition root), `build`
(the dangerous core), `html-postprocess` (pure helpers: hash routing, theme
normalization, external stripping, meta), `themes`, `auth`, `ratelimit`,
`openapi` (route registry → OpenAPI 3.1), `metrics`, `multipart`. The root
`server.mjs` is a thin entry. OpenAPI is generated from the route registry so docs
can't drift from the implementation.

---

## 10. Troubleshooting

- **HTTP 500 "build failed"** — the deck has invalid Slidev syntax, references an
  uninstalled Slidev `theme:` (now normalized away — update to a current build),
  or uses an uninstalled icon collection (`<carbon-…/>` etc. are fine; exotic ones
  need installing + a rebuild). The server logs the real `slidev build` error.
- **HTTP 401** — auth is enabled; send `Authorization: Bearer <key>`.
- **HTTP 413** — upload exceeds `SLIDEKIT_MAX_BODY_BYTES` (default 1 MiB).
- **HTTP 429** — over `SLIDEKIT_RATE_LIMIT_MAX`; honor `Retry-After`.
- **HTTP 503/504** — build queue full / build timed out; retry or raise the caps.
- **Fonts/links loaded from the network** — set `fonts: { provider: none }` in the
  deck; the server strips external `<link>`s, but `provider: none` is cleaner.
- **Output not rendering offline / presenter not working** — hash routing is
  injected automatically; open the file directly and use `…#/presenter/1`.
